name: Ed-Med
description_short: Ed-Med Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution established in 1959, serving over 2,100 members across the education sector from its main office in Ogdensburg, NY.
description_long: Ed-Med Federal Credit Union was established in 1959 and is headquartered in Ogdensburg, New York. As a not-for-profit credit union, it operates under a cooperative membership model designed to serve the financial needs of its community members. The institution manages over $30.2 million in assets and maintains a single branch location with one ATM. Ed-Med offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services typical of a full-service credit union. Members can access checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, IRAs/KEOGH accounts, wire transfers, mortgage loans, and online bill pay. The credit union also provides digital banking through online banking platforms and mobile apps available on both iOS and Android devices, alongside e-statements and financial counseling services. A key distinguishing feature is Ed-Med's focus on serving educational organizations and their employees. The credit union caters primarily to multiple educational sector organizations, which shapes its membership eligibility requirements and product offerings. As a not-for-profit institution, Ed-Med emphasizes competitive rates and lower fees compared to traditional for-profit banks. Current published rates include 5.00% APR for new vehicle loans, 5.50% for used vehicle loans, and 8.00% for other unsecured loans. Ed-Med serves a relatively modest membership base of 2,100+ members through a lean operational structure with over 3 dedicated employees as of 2026. The limited branch network (1 location) and single ATM may restrict accessibility for members outside the Ogdensburg area. While the credit union provides standard financial services competently, its specialized focus on the education sector and geographic limitations mean it is best suited for eligible members in or near northern New York.
pros: Established institution with 65+ years of history and stability (founded 1959)
Competitive rates: 5.00% APR on new auto loans and 8.00% on unsecured loans
Full-service offerings including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, CDs, and IRAs
Not-for-profit structure emphasizing lower fees and member-focused rates
Digital banking access via online platform and mobile apps (iOS/Android)
Direct financial counseling services available to members
Member community Q&A forum and financial tip resources
Online bill pay and e-statement capabilities for digital account management
cons: Severely limited branch network with only 1 physical location in Ogdensburg, NY and 1 ATM
Restrictive membership eligibility tied primarily to educational organizations, not open to general public
No current reviews on the profile page, limiting transparent member feedback
Small asset base ($30.2M) and modest membership (2,100) may limit lending flexibility or product innovation
Geographic isolation in northern upstate New York limits accessibility for non-local members
best_for: Employees and eligible members of educational organizations in or near Ogdensburg, NY
Members seeking a stable, not-for-profit alternative to larger commercial banks
Education sector workers looking for competitive auto and personal loan rates
Local members preferring personalized community banking with financial counseling
diagnosis: Ed-Med Federal Credit Union is best for eligible employees of educational organizations based in or near Ogdensburg, NY who value the stability and member-focused approach of a not-for-profit credit union. The primary caveat is membership eligibility restricted to the education sector and severe geographic limitations due to a single branch location, making it inaccessible for most consumers outside the organization's service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
